_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Waterford is the lightest Munster record: a Munster quarter-final loss to Tipperary followed by two heavy Tailteann Cup defeats (a five-goal loss to London, then a loss to Sligo). Three championship games, all defeats.
_meta.verification: All scorelines and venues verified against the official RTE and Connacht/Munster GAA results tables and primary reports. Scorer detail is sparse in the available coverage for Waterford and honest nulls are used where the full list was not in hand.

manager.name: Ephie Fitzgerald
manager.note: Manager per the 2026 Munster championship listing. Oversaw a difficult season: out of Munster at the quarter-final and out of the Tailteann Cup after two games, conceding eight goals across the two Tailteann defeats.

league.note: Waterford competed in Division 4 of the National League. Exact final position not captured here.

source.snippet: those that did not reach the final of their provincial championship and were not among the seven highest-ranked teams at the end of the 2026 National Football League.

scorers.note: midfielder; caught a dropping Power effort and fired low to the net

results.note: A five-point home Munster quarter-final loss to Tipperary (the lowest-scoring game of the 2026 Munster series). Full scorer list not captured; Tipperary's file carries the opposite perspective. To be filled on a verification pass.

source.snippet: First Round 10 May Waterford 0-10 5-12 London Cappoquin Logistics Fraher Field

results.note: A chastening 17-point home Tailteann opener defeat, conceding five goals to London (a landmark day for the Exiles). Waterford goalkeeper Paudie Hunt fumbled an early shot for the first London goal. This Round 1 loss dropped Waterford into the Round 2B knockout. Full scorer list not captured; London's file carries the opposite perspective.

results.note: Elimination game. Despite scoring three goals, Waterford were beaten by eight as Sligo racked up 0-29 (Lee Deignan 0-11 for Sligo). Ended Waterford's season at the Round 2B stage. Full scorer list not captured; Sligo's file carries the opposite perspective and the Sligo scorer detail. To be filled on a verification pass.

collection_meta.notes: Waterford is the lightest of the five buildable Munster records and a straightforwardly bleak one: three games, three defeats, eighteen goals conceded across the campaign (including eight in the two Tailteann games). No standout individual scorer emerged in the captured coverage. The opposing perspectives (Tipperary, London, Sligo) all live in their respective files with fuller detail. Editorially, Waterford and the other Munster Tailteann strugglers are capsule-weight, not chapter-weight. A verification pass should add Waterford's own scorer lists from primary reports where they exist.
